name: CERT: Undecidable Rule Manually Reviewed
description: Potential violations of undecidable rules (not fully checkable by static analysis) are manually reviewed.
rationale: SEI CERT: some rules are undecidable; a sound analyzer over-approximates, and remaining cases require manual review to confirm conformance.
source: SEI CERT Coding Standards (Carnegie Mellon Software Engineering Institute) — CERT C and CERT C++ Secure Coding Standards, with the CERT risk-assessment methodology (Severity × Likelihood × Remediation Cost → Priority → Level L1/L2/L3). https://wiki.sei.cmu.edu/confluence/display/seccode
